title: Make connector snapshot identity and compatibility observable
goal: Expose which connector snapshot is actually serving ChatGPT, which server and agent contracts it advertises and whether that snapshot is structurally compatible with the deployed Grabowski runtime and canonical operator-entry policy without claiming model comprehension or universal client compliance.

acceptance.assertion: Runtime evidence includes connector build identity, connector profile, visible tool-catalog digest, server tool-catalog digest, agent-instructions digest, canonical operator-entry digest and observation timestamp.
A typed structural result distinguishes compatible, stale, incompatible, unobservable and malformed snapshots with explicit reason codes and compared digests.
Unknown or stale connector identity cannot establish tool-surface or instruction-contract compatibility or suppress warnings, while read-only diagnostics remain available.
Deployment, connector restart and client reconnection tests detect old snapshots after a runtime, profile or instruction-contract change.

The surface does not expose or imply a universal client_compliant result, model comprehension, hidden intent or correct future behavior; those remain separate from structural snapshot compatibility and from GRABOWSKI-OPERATOR-SURFACE-V1-T047 behavior evidence.

metadata.observation: grabowski_contract_drift reported connector_snapshot_observable=false and structural client snapshot compatibility could not be determined.

t017_followup_evidence.observation: After deploying a new Grabowski typed tool, the already-open ChatGPT session did not expose the new tool in its frozen function schema, while a fresh independent MCP client discovered the complete 155-tool runtime contract and executed the new review tool successfully. The connector snapshot was subsequently rebound and reported fresh/matched.
t017_followup_evidence.interpretation: This is additional evidence for T002 drift/reconnection scope, not a failure of T017 fresh-session adoption and not a reason to create a duplicate task.
